title: stackhousia umbellata
intro: Stackhousia umbellata is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae, commonly known as the grey-eyed yellow bells, native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Stackhousia umbellata are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
